releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.55
releases.excerpt: Drop-to-rename first run. New installs now put file dropping and rename previews front and center, so you can try NameQuick by dropping files before setting up folders or automations.
highlights: Drop-to-rename first run. New installs now put file dropping and rename previews front and center, so you can try NameQuick by dropping files before setting up folders or automations.
Undo support for sidebar folder moves. Files moved by dropping them onto sidebar folders now appear in Recent Renames and can be moved back from the same history flow.
Clearer All Files empty state. The empty library view now shows a clearer drop card, a Choose Files action, and quick paths to add a watch folder or open Presets.
Safer Quick Process drops. Quick Process now makes it clearer when a staged batch is being worked on from disk only, and warns before replacing an existing batch with a new drop.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.54
releases.excerpt: Clearer payment recovery for Managed subscriptions. If a Managed payment does not go through, NameQuick now shows an update-payment path in the app and Settings instead of sending you through a generic upgrade screen.
highlights: Clearer payment recovery for Managed subscriptions. If a Managed payment does not go through, NameQuick now shows an update-payment path in the app and Settings instead of sending you through a generic upgrade screen.
More responsive license refresh. The Refresh button in Settings now shows when NameQuick is checking your license, so it is clearer that your request is in progress.
Easier template setup. The template editor now makes it clearer that fields find data, while the filename builder controls what gets included in the final name.
Fixed failed Managed payments being treated like active access. NameQuick now blocks Managed features until billing is fixed, while keeping your account recoverable.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.53
releases.excerpt: Fixed Self-Managed trial activation getting stuck after checkout. NameQuick now recovers more reliably when checkout finishes but license confirmation takes longer than expected.
highlights: Fixed Self-Managed trial activation getting stuck after checkout. NameQuick now recovers more reliably when checkout finishes but license confirmation takes longer than expected.
Fixed unclear recovery when activation cannot be confirmed. Settings now points you back to the right activation step instead of making a paid-but-stuck purchase look like a missing license key.

Fixed abandoned checkout sessions staying in progress. NameQuick now resolves canceled or unverifiable activation attempts more cleanly, so the app does not keep waiting on a checkout that is no longer active.
Fixed repeat activation retries after restart. Pending Self-Managed trial activation now resumes with better boundaries and stops stale retry loops when the license cannot be verified.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.52
releases.excerpt: Fixed paid upgrades from trials getting stuck. Upgrading from a trial to a paid Managed plan now completes more reliably and refreshes credits sooner.
highlights: Fixed paid upgrades from trials getting stuck. Upgrading from a trial to a paid Managed plan now completes more reliably and refreshes credits sooner.
Fixed Self-Managed upgrade sheets closing unexpectedly. Upgrading from a Self-Managed trial now keeps the upgrade sheet open when your trial state refreshes.
Fixed onboarding license-step loops. The onboarding flow no longer keeps reopening the same license step while license status updates are settling.

Fixed interrupted Watch Folder scans. NameQuick now recovers abandoned folder catalog scans after restart and clears stalled processing state more safely, so automatic folder work and content search can recover instead of staying stuck.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.51
releases.excerpt: Clearer MLX runtime setup. When a selected MLX model is ready but the runtime still needs installation, Settings now shows an inline setup prompt with an Install MLX runtime action.
highlights: Clearer MLX runtime setup. When a selected MLX model is ready but the runtime still needs installation, Settings now shows an inline setup prompt with an Install MLX runtime action.
Clearer empty Watch Folder state. The sidebar now says No folders when no watched folders are configured, and file locations are easier for help and accessibility tools to identify.

Fixed stale Watch Folder files appearing in All Files. Removing, resetting, or repairing Watch Folders now keeps the library, search index, and selection counts aligned so old tracked items do not keep showing up.
Fixed Watch Folder views falling back to the wrong scope. Folder-based views now stay limited to the active watched folders instead of accidentally showing the broader library after stale selections.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.50
releases.excerpt: Camera metadata for templates, custom prompts, and rules. You can now use Date Taken, Camera Make, and Camera Model from image metadata when building photo-focused naming workflows.
highlights: Camera metadata for templates, custom prompts, and rules. You can now use Date Taken, Camera Make, and Camera Model from image metadata when building photo-focused naming workflows.
Faster, clearer MLX model installs. MLX model downloads now show more accurate progress, speed, and ETA, download large model files in parallel, and check available disk space before starting.
MLX models are ready sooner after install. When you install the selected MLX model from Settings, NameQuick can warm it in the background so the first rename does not have to wait as long.

Clearer plan management in Settings. About now shows fewer irrelevant license controls, uses clearer upgrade wording, and offers a Self-Managed choice from the managed plan flow when that switch is available.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.49
releases.excerpt: Updated Managed credit tiers. In-app Managed plan details now align with the current 500, 2,000, 5,000, and 10,000 credit options, so plan and trial screens match the current subscription ladder.
highlights: Updated Managed credit tiers. In-app Managed plan details now align with the current 500, 2,000, 5,000, and 10,000 credit options, so plan and trial screens match the current subscription ladder.
Clearer self-managed wording. Trial, upgrade, and account surfaces now use simpler wording around self-managed access and Managed subscriptions.
Better queued-processing recovery. Temporary broker or provider rejections now stay retryable instead of looking like final processing failures.
Clearer setup and access stops. Jobs that need setup, license access, or policy attention are separated from ordinary retryable work so the queue reports the right next step.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.48
releases.excerpt: More practical template presets. New starters for bank statements, invoices, receipts, admin forms, travel bookings, insurance documents, and contracts make it faster to build repeatable naming workflows.
highlights: More practical template presets. New starters for bank statements, invoices, receipts, admin forms, travel bookings, insurance documents, and contracts make it faster to build repeatable naming workflows.
More focused rule templates. Rule starters now cover common document routing needs like statements, invoices, receipts, admin documents, insurance files, travel bookings, and contracts.
Better template onboarding. The template lesson now shows the full preset catalog, so you can start from the workflow that matches your files instead of choosing from a shortened list.
Cleaner starter filenames. Built-in template presets now use more focused fields and consistent separators, making generated names easier to scan and sort.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.47
releases.excerpt: More practical template presets. New starters for bank statements, invoices, receipts, admin forms, travel bookings, insurance documents, and contracts make it faster to build repeatable naming workflows.
highlights: More practical template presets. New starters for bank statements, invoices, receipts, admin forms, travel bookings, insurance documents, and contracts make it faster to build repeatable naming workflows.
More focused rule templates. Rule starters now cover common document routing needs like statements, invoices, receipts, admin documents, insurance files, travel bookings, and contracts.
Better template onboarding. The template lesson now shows the full preset catalog, so you can start from the workflow that matches your files instead of choosing from a shortened list.
Cleaner starter filenames. Built-in template presets now use more focused fields and consistent separators, making generated names easier to scan and sort.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.46
releases.excerpt: Review hold for automatic renames. A new setting can send watched-folder and automatic rename suggestions to Review before files are changed, so hands-off workflows can still have a check step.
highlights: Review hold for automatic renames. A new setting can send watched-folder and automatic rename suggestions to Review before files are changed, so hands-off workflows can still have a check step.
Move-back actions for rule-organized files. Recent Renames now shows files moved, trashed, or archived by rules, with Move back or Put back actions where reversal is supported.
Safer first-run renaming. Fresh installs now start in Review mode, so new users see proposed filenames before NameQuick writes changes to disk.
More predictable watched-folder setup. NameQuick handles existing files during watch-folder setup more reliably, with clearer baselines for future arrivals.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.45
releases.excerpt: New rule templates for common document workflows. NameQuick now includes more starting points for everyday document organization, making it faster to create useful rules without building every condition from scratch.
highlights: New rule templates for common document workflows. NameQuick now includes more starting points for everyday document organization, making it faster to create useful rules without building every condition from scratch.
Apple-native document support for AI renaming. Pages, Numbers, Keynote, and RTFD files can now be read for naming workflows instead of being treated like unsupported files.
More capable local Smart Rename. Local AI naming now keeps the generated name grounded in the file’s actual text and sends uncertain details to review instead of guessing.
Smarter custom prompt authoring. Custom prompts now preserve more of the naming plan you wrote, so repeated workflows behave more consistently across different files.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.44
releases.excerpt: Apply saved rules to files that are already in watched folders. After saving a rule, NameQuick can now offer to run it on matching existing files, so you do not have to wait for new files to arrive before a rule takes effect.
highlights: Apply saved rules to files that are already in watched folders. After saving a rule, NameQuick can now offer to run it on matching existing files, so you do not have to wait for new files to arrive before a rule takes effect.

Choose whether a move rule renames a file or moves it into a folder. The rules editor now makes this choice explicit, which helps destinations such as Reports.pages or 2026.backups behave the way you expect.

Test rules on demand. The rules editor now has a clear Test button with readable states, so you can check a rule against sample files when you are ready instead of relying on silent background testing.
More reliable move rules. Folder moves, nested destinations, counters, and files that are already in the right folder are handled more consistently.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.43
releases.excerpt: Search inside file contents. NameQuick can now find text inside PDFs, images, scanned documents, and supported document and text files, so files can show up even when the matching words are not in the file name.
highlights: Search inside file contents. NameQuick can now find text inside PDFs, images, scanned documents, and supported document and text files, so files can show up even when the matching words are not in the file name.
Clearer content search progress. Rebuilding, updating, and cleaning the content index now show visible status in Settings and the main window, so you can tell when NameQuick is still checking files.
Newer local Gemma 4 models. NameQuick now recommends newer Gemma 4 local models that are designed to use less memory, making local processing a better fit for more Macs.
More reliable local model setup. NameQuick is better at choosing and preparing local models for your Mac, including clearer handling for Gemma, MLX, LM Studio, and Ollama workflows.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.42
releases.excerpt: Trial credits now behave correctly for Self-Managed batches. You can start a selected batch while trial credits remain, even when more files are selected than credits left.
highlights: Trial credits now behave correctly for Self-Managed batches. You can start a selected batch while trial credits remain, even when more files are selected than credits left.
Local Gemma custom prompts are steadier on CPU-only Macs. NameQuick now avoids a heavier local-model path when custom prompts run without GPU acceleration.

Fixed trial credit batches opening the unlock sheet too early. Self-Managed trial users can now start processing while credits remain. NameQuick stops cleanly when trial credits run out instead of asking to unlock before the first rename starts.
Fixed trial-credit exhaustion during a batch. When the remaining trial balance reaches zero, NameQuick blocks the next model request before it runs.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.41
releases.excerpt: Quick Process now handles one-off folders and files. Drop a folder or a few files, choose what to rename, and run a temporary batch without setting up a Watch Folder.
highlights: Quick Process now handles one-off folders and files. Drop a folder or a few files, choose what to rename, and run a temporary batch without setting up a Watch Folder.
New rename mode menu. Choose Rename automatically or Review before renaming from the main toolbar More menu before starting the next batch.
Audit view became History and is much more useful. Past renames now appear in a searchable timeline with batch grouping, file details, issue counts, and clearer undo or redo actions.
Rules are easier to build. The new guided rule composer turns rule setup into a step-by-step sentence with searchable rule management.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.40
releases.excerpt: More reliable Self-Managed license recovery. NameQuick now handles older Self-Managed licenses more consistently after restarting, reinstalling, or signing in again on the same Mac.
highlights: More reliable Self-Managed license recovery. NameQuick now handles older Self-Managed licenses more consistently after restarting, reinstalling, or signing in again on the same Mac.
Clearer device-limit recovery. When a license needs a device slot freed up, NameQuick now keeps the right recovery state visible instead of getting stuck on a loading screen.
Safer subscription credit display. Subscription credits now appear only after NameQuick has confirmed the active subscription details, keeping syncing states clear while billing data loads.
Fixed Self-Managed licenses getting stuck on launch. Older licenses should no longer hang during startup while NameQuick checks activation status.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.39
releases.excerpt: Local model cleanup. You can now remove installed Ollama and MLX models from NameQuick, with a clear “Removing…” status while cleanup is in progress.
highlights: Local model cleanup. You can now remove installed Ollama and MLX models from NameQuick, with a clear “Removing…” status while cleanup is in progress.

Smarter local model recommendations. NameQuick now groups local models by how well they fit everyday renaming, images, scanned PDFs, and advanced workflows, making it easier to choose the right Ollama, LM Studio, or MLX model.
Updated Gemini support. NameQuick now uses Google’s current Gemini Flash model by default and keeps Gemini-powered renaming aligned with the latest available API.

More reliable templates and custom prompts. Templates and custom prompts now handle dates, separators, suffixes, capitalization, and formatting instructions more consistently across cloud and local AI providers.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.37
releases.excerpt: Improved custom prompts with local AI providers. Templates and custom prompts now handle formatting instructions more reliably when using Ollama, LM Studio, or MLX.
highlights: Improved custom prompts with local AI providers. Templates and custom prompts now handle formatting instructions more reliably when using Ollama, LM Studio, or MLX.
Polished local AI controls. The active provider card and process mode menu now feel clearer and more consistent.

Fixed local AI template failures caused by formatting instructions. Date formats, separators, and suffixes like Euro should no longer be treated as missing fields when processing templates or custom prompts.
Fixed billing portal links. Account and billing links now open the correct customer portal instead of leading to a missing page.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.36
releases.excerpt: Fixed startup for some self-managed license users. NameQuick now opens the right license repair screen instead of getting stuck on “Checking license” when a paid self-managed license needs device access repaired.
highlights: Fixed startup for some self-managed license users. NameQuick now opens the right license repair screen instead of getting stuck on “Checking license” when a paid self-managed license needs device access repaired.
Improved repair guidance for self-managed licenses. When a license reaches its device limit, NameQuick now keeps the account correctly recognized as self-managed and shows the right recovery actions.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.35
releases.excerpt: Fixed managed Gemini processing for images and documents. Files should now process normally instead of failing before AI analysis with a provider request error.
highlights: Fixed managed Gemini processing for images and documents. Files should now process normally instead of failing before AI analysis with a provider request error.

Improved error guidance for provider request problems. When a provider rejects a request format, NameQuick now explains that updating the app or contacting support is more useful than changing your template.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.34
releases.excerpt: Made credit updates more reliable for paid plans. NameQuick now handles subscription credit renewals more consistently, especially when account details arrive in stages.
highlights: Made credit updates more reliable for paid plans. NameQuick now handles subscription credit renewals more consistently, especially when account details arrive in stages.
Improved credit status during account setup. Trial and paid-plan setup now keep showing the right setup state while NameQuick finishes checking your account.
Made saved templates more reliable. Templates and custom prompts are better protected during settings resets, so saved naming workflows should remain available.
Fixed trial setup showing the wrong credit state. Trial activation should now stay in the correct setup flow instead of briefly showing unrelated credit or license messaging.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.33
releases.excerpt: A clearer AI provider settings experience. The AI settings page now highlights the active provider, available models, setup actions, and local processing choices in one place.
highlights: A clearer AI provider settings experience. The AI settings page now highlights the active provider, available models, setup actions, and local processing choices in one place.

Better recovery help for local AI providers. When LM Studio, Ollama, or MLX needs attention, NameQuick now shows focused next steps and support options instead of leaving the problem hidden in the status bar.
Diagnostics export for support. You can now create a support-ready diagnostics report when a processing issue needs a closer look.
Made MLX model loading easier to understand. Downloaded MLX models now ask you to load the model, while runtime installation remains a separate setup step.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.32
releases.excerpt: Improved custom prompts with local AI providers. Filename suggestions from LM Studio and Ollama now follow a more reliable step-by-step flow, so custom instructions are less likely to leak into the final name.
highlights: Improved custom prompts with local AI providers. Filename suggestions from LM Studio and Ollama now follow a more reliable step-by-step flow, so custom instructions are less likely to leak into the final name.
Fixed confusing local AI filename failures. When a local provider cannot return a usable filename, NameQuick now shows a clearer problem instead of silently accepting malformed output.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.30
releases.excerpt: Improved license recovery after reinstalling NameQuick. Self-Managed licenses can now reconnect to the same Mac more reliably instead of getting stuck on device activation.
highlights: Improved license recovery after reinstalling NameQuick. Self-Managed licenses can now reconnect to the same Mac more reliably instead of getting stuck on device activation.
Made trial activation more reliable. NameQuick now checks existing account access more consistently before asking you to start checkout again.
Fixed Self-Managed trial activation getting stuck. Existing Self-Managed trial users should now be able to activate with their email instead of being blocked at the license screen.
Fixed subscription trials looping back to checkout. Active trial accounts should now unlock from their existing account access even when checkout confirmation is delayed.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.29
releases.excerpt: Made subscription sign-in more reliable. NameQuick now handles more account details from the billing system, so active customers are less likely to be asked to start checkout again.
highlights: Made subscription sign-in more reliable. NameQuick now handles more account details from the billing system, so active customers are less likely to be asked to start checkout again.
Improved account checks after checkout. Returning to the app after starting a managed AI trial now handles existing account access more carefully.

Fixed a bug where active subscription trials could fail to verify. Customers with an active managed AI trial should now be recognized correctly instead of seeing a connection-style verification error.
Fixed a bug that could mix up managed AI and self-managed access. NameQuick now keeps self-managed provider choices separate from managed AI subscriptions more consistently.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.28
releases.excerpt: Made saved templates more reliable. Your templates and custom prompts are now more likely to stay available after app updates, relaunches, or Finder Quick Action use.
highlights: Made saved templates more reliable. Your templates and custom prompts are now more likely to stay available after app updates, relaunches, or Finder Quick Action use.
Improved template syncing with Finder Quick Actions. Templates used from Finder now stay better aligned with the ones you saved in the app.
Fixed the Automation permission row getting stuck. First-run users now see the Grant Access option when Automation permission has not been decided yet, and the row updates right after they respond.
Fixed a bug where templates and custom prompts could disappear. Saved naming templates and custom prompts should now remain available instead of unexpectedly resetting.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.27
releases.excerpt: Added shareable processing reports. You can now copy a clear report from a processing run, making it easier to review what happened or share details when asking for help.
highlights: Added shareable processing reports. You can now copy a clear report from a processing run, making it easier to review what happened or share details when asking for help.
Added clearer processing issue cards. NameQuick now groups processing problems into easier-to-scan cards with clearer labels and next steps.
Made local AI limits easier to manage. Advanced budget controls for Ollama, LM Studio, and MLX are now tucked behind a cleaner disclosure section while keeping your saved settings intact.
Improved local provider guardrails. NameQuick now checks local model limits more consistently before sending work, helping prevent oversized requests and keeping provider behavior predictable.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.26
releases.excerpt: Added stronger control for keeping MLX models loaded. When you choose to keep a local MLX model loaded, NameQuick now treats that as an app-level promise while the app is open.
highlights: Added stronger control for keeping MLX models loaded. When you choose to keep a local MLX model loaded, NameQuick now treats that as an app-level promise while the app is open.

Made local model wording easier to understand. MLX controls now use familiar language like “Load model,” “Unload model,” and “Keep model loaded,” matching how Ollama and LM Studio users already think about local models.

Improved MLX setup and readiness checks. NameQuick now handles the bundled runtime environment more carefully, checks that the runtime is actually installed before starting, and keeps setup progress from being overwritten mid-install.
Made the MLX settings control more compact. The keep-loaded switch now takes up less space while staying easy to scan in the local AI settings.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.25
releases.excerpt: Added an Audit log for rename runs. You can now review recent processing details, understand why a filename needed recovery, and clear the log when you no longer need it.
highlights: Added an Audit log for rename runs. You can now review recent processing details, understand why a filename needed recovery, and clear the log when you no longer need it.
Added clearer field review for templates. Template tests now highlight which extracted details look confident and which ones may need a closer look before you use the result.
Made custom template filenames more reliable. NameQuick now handles templates that combine fixed fields, sample invoice wording, and custom instructions with fewer formatting surprises.

Improved recovery guidance for missing file details. When a rename cannot be completed cleanly, NameQuick gives more specific next steps so you can update the instructions or source file and try again.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.24
releases.excerpt: Added stronger local AI setup and loading. NameQuick can now install and load local models through more of the provider’s own app experience, including LM Studio, with clearer progress while setup is still running.
highlights: Added stronger local AI setup and loading. NameQuick can now install and load local models through more of the provider’s own app experience, including LM Studio, with clearer progress while setup is still running.

Added recovery guidance when file details are missing. When a rename request needs information that is not visible in the file, NameQuick can now explain what is missing and suggest a practical next step instead of returning a confusing filename.

Made local provider setup more reliable. Local AI readiness checks now better distinguish app availability, endpoint health, model status, and runtime state so NameQuick can choose the right recovery path.
Improved local model downloads. LM Studio downloads now show clearer progress and avoid switching to a local command-line fallback when you are using a remote LM Studio server.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.23
releases.excerpt: Added a more dependable local AI runtime package. The bundled local model setup now focuses on the text, image, and PDF workflows NameQuick uses, so local AI features can start with fewer optional components getting in the way.
highlights: Added a more dependable local AI runtime package. The bundled local model setup now focuses on the text, image, and PDF workflows NameQuick uses, so local AI features can start with fewer optional components getting in the way.
Made local AI readiness easier to understand. NameQuick now separates setup, start, model, and runtime health states so the app can point you to the right next step.
Made local model extraction more resilient. NameQuick can retry structured answers when a local model cuts off or repeats itself, while still rejecting malformed data.
Improved update delivery for larger releases. App downloads and updates are now handled in a way that stays reliable as bundled local AI support grows.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.22
releases.excerpt: Refreshed the installer artwork. The drag-to-Applications window now uses updated release packaging art.
highlights: Refreshed the installer artwork. The drag-to-Applications window now uses updated release packaging art.
Made MLX local model readiness easier to control. NameQuick now has clearer Start, Stop, and Keep Ready behavior, and avoids starting the runtime just because you view settings.
Made custom template and freeform filenames more reliable. Local filename prompts now use a safer flow that extracts file facts first, then builds filenames from those facts.
Improved Ollama PDF processing. Difficult PDFs can finish the rename first while optional metadata is handled separately when needed.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.21
releases.excerpt: Fixed a release packaging issue that could make the app look damaged after download. NameQuick now ships a relocatable MLX runtime bundle that passes Gatekeeper checks.
highlights: Fixed a release packaging issue that could make the app look damaged after download. NameQuick now ships a relocatable MLX runtime bundle that passes Gatekeeper checks.
Fixed website downloads that could return a tiny invalid disk image. Download links now resolve to a valid DMG payload.
Added stricter release validation before publish. The release pipeline now checks MLX runtime symlink safety plus codesign --strict and Gatekeeper assessment on the exported app.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.19
releases.excerpt: Fixed watch-folder cases where real arrivals could be skipped as too old. Files moved in by automation tools are now recognized more consistently.
highlights: Fixed watch-folder cases where real arrivals could be skipped as too old. Files moved in by automation tools are now recognized more consistently.
Fixed reconciliation edge cases during monitor stop and restart cycles. Deferred scans and replay behavior now converge more reliably.
Fixed startup database adoption edge cases across app versions. Launch-time state is less likely to fail when older store paths are present.
Fixed currency consistency gaps between checkout and paywall flows. Pricing and billing context now stay aligned more reliably.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.18
releases.excerpt: Managed plan upgrades now feel more direct and clearer. When you run out of credits on a managed plan, NameQuick can guide you through upgrading with clearer billing choices and more localized pricing details.
highlights: Managed plan upgrades now feel more direct and clearer. When you run out of credits on a managed plan, NameQuick can guide you through upgrading with clearer billing choices and more localized pricing details.
Account, license, and access state stay in sync more reliably. Startup, checkout, restore, and relaunch flows are less likely to leave the app showing the wrong access state.

BYOK and managed access paths are handled more consistently. NameQuick does a better job keeping trial, paid, and managed states on the right path instead of drifting into the wrong upgrade or billing flow.
Billing and upgrade screens are easier to understand. Pricing, confirmation, and plan selection around subscription changes now feel more explicit and less guessy.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.17
releases.excerpt: Improved account and license syncing reliability. NameQuick now uses an updated backend path for customer access and license checks.
highlights: Improved account and license syncing reliability. NameQuick now uses an updated backend path for customer access and license checks.
Existing paid access restores more consistently. Subscription state, customer lookups, and usage syncing now run through the updated licensing flow.
Improved security around billing and licensing infrastructure. Sensitive billing credentials are now handled entirely server-side.
Updated release tooling for the new licensing flow. Internal build and support scripts now align with the backend-managed path.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.16
releases.excerpt: License and access state now recover more cleanly after checkout, restore, and relaunch. NameQuick does a better job carrying your activation state forward without requiring extra cleanup from you.
highlights: License and access state now recover more cleanly after checkout, restore, and relaunch. NameQuick does a better job carrying your activation state forward without requiring extra cleanup from you.
BYOK access feels more reliable across trial and paid states. The app now keeps the right access path and avoids drifting into the wrong upgrade or billing flow.
Onboarding progress stays in sync more consistently. Reopening the app is less likely to leave setup guidance out of step with where you actually are.
Quick actions and toolbar status feel steadier during a session. Popovers, action states, and startup UI now behave more predictably.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.15
releases.excerpt: A new onboarding flow helps you get to your first working setup faster. First-run guidance is more structured and does a better job walking you through permissions, setup, and template creation.
highlights: A new onboarding flow helps you get to your first working setup faster. First-run guidance is more structured and does a better job walking you through permissions, setup, and template creation.
Build custom prompts and templates in chat. You can now create and refine naming setups through a more conversational chat-first flow instead of configuring everything manually upfront.
LM Studio models can be loaded in-app. Browse, load, and manage LM Studio models without leaving NameQuick.
File dates are available as template fallback values. Use created, modified, or accessed dates when a document is missing stronger metadata.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.14
releases.excerpt: A new onboarding flow helps you get to your first working setup faster. First-run guidance is more structured and does a better job walking you through permissions, setup, and template creation.
highlights: A new onboarding flow helps you get to your first working setup faster. First-run guidance is more structured and does a better job walking you through permissions, setup, and template creation.
Build custom prompts and templates in chat. You can now create and refine naming setups through a more conversational chat-first flow instead of configuring everything manually upfront.
LM Studio models can be loaded in-app. Browse, load, and manage LM Studio models without leaving NameQuick.
File dates are available as template fallback values. Use created, modified, or accessed dates when a document is missing stronger metadata.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.13
releases.excerpt: Metadata results are now more consistent across AI providers. Filename, tags, and description generation now behaves more predictably when you switch between supported providers.
highlights: Metadata results are now more consistent across AI providers. Filename, tags, and description generation now behaves more predictably when you switch between supported providers.
The update banner now includes a full "What's New" view. You can expand details in-app, review highlights, and choose to install now or be reminded later.
Metadata handling is more reliable when AI responses are incomplete. NameQuick now recovers more often instead of failing, and continues with filename-only output when needed.
Template placeholders are more reliable across languages and regions. Date placeholders are more stable, and field matching now handles accented characters more accurately.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.12
releases.excerpt: OpenRouter is now available as a BYOK provider. Bring your own OpenRouter API key to access hundreds of models from a single provider.
highlights: OpenRouter is now available as a BYOK provider. Bring your own OpenRouter API key to access hundreds of models from a single provider.
Presets sort alphabetically. The Presets list (formerly "Custom Prompts") now sorts A–Z by default for easier browsing.
Japanese and CJK filenames no longer get mangled. Smart rename no longer inserts separators between every character in CJK output.
Expired BYOK trials stay usable. An expired trial no longer hard-blocks the app — you can still browse files and explore before upgrading.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.11
releases.excerpt: BYOK trial email login works reliably. We now recognize trial entitlements even without an active subscription, so your BYOK trial email won’t get rejected.
highlights: BYOK trial email login works reliably. We now recognize trial entitlements even without an active subscription, so your BYOK trial email won’t get rejected.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.10
releases.excerpt: BYOK license no longer lost after Keychain hiccups. Temporary Keychain access issues no longer clear your saved API keys.
highlights: BYOK license no longer lost after Keychain hiccups. Temporary Keychain access issues no longer clear your saved API keys.
Offline startup works with newer subscription plans. No longer requires an internet connection on every launch.
Onboarding shows accurate key status. Setup flow correctly reflects when keys are actually usable.
File list updates more reliably. Changes to tags and metadata now refresh immediately.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.09
releases.excerpt: Per-field required/optional settings. Each extraction field now has its own required/optional dropdown, replacing the global toggle. This gives you precise control over which fields must have values for a rename to proceed.
highlights: Per-field required/optional settings. Each extraction field now has its own required/optional dropdown, replacing the global toggle. This gives you precise control over which fields must have values for a rename to proceed.
In-app update notifications. A banner now appears when an update is available, with quick access to install or view the changelog.

Redesigned AI provider cards. Provider cards now use accordion behavior—expanding one collapses others. Selection is integrated directly into each card, and the app auto-selects a valid provider when you save a new API key.
More reliable watch folder processing. Files added during brief monitoring pauses are now caught by a follow-up scan.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.07
releases.excerpt: German language support. The app is now fully localized in German. Language follows your system preference. Feedback on the translations is welcome—reach out via email.
highlights: German language support. The app is now fully localized in German. Language follows your system preference. Feedback on the translations is welcome—reach out via email.

Credit tracking is more reliable across app restarts. Fixed an issue where credit usage could be lost or reset unexpectedly when the app restarted during a billing period. Your usage now persists correctly even if sync timing varies.
Monthly credit rollover timing is more accurate. Improved scheduling so credit allowances reset at the correct time when a new billing period begins.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.06
releases.excerpt: Local text-only models now work with text PDFs. Ollama and LM Studio text-only models can process PDFs that contain extractable text. Clear error messages appear when a vision model is needed for images or scanned PDFs.
highlights: Local text-only models now work with text PDFs. Ollama and LM Studio text-only models can process PDFs that contain extractable text. Clear error messages appear when a vision model is needed for images or scanned PDFs.
Quick access to support email. Help menu and Settings now include direct links to reach support.
SVG files process correctly for subscription users. Fixed an issue where SVG, AI, and EPS files failed when processed through the subscription path.
Rules action editors stay visible. Tag and comment action editors no longer disappear unexpectedly. Archive folder picker now correctly shows the base folder with relative path.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.05
releases.excerpt: Subscription credits now display correctly. Fixed an issue where credits showed as 0 for subscription customers despite having available balance.
highlights: Subscription credits now display correctly. Fixed an issue where credits showed as 0 for subscription customers despite having available balance.
Shift-click selection works reliably. File table selection range now behaves correctly when shift-clicking.
Fixed memory leaks during inference. Resolved memory growth when processing many files.
More reliable watch folder processing.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.04
releases.excerpt: Subscription credits now display correctly. Fixed an issue where some subscription customers saw 0 credits available despite having an active plan.
highlights: Subscription credits now display correctly. Fixed an issue where some subscription customers saw 0 credits available despite having an active plan.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.03
releases.excerpt: Local model downloads in Settings – Local Providers now list recommended Ollama and LM Studio models with hardware guidance and quick download or copy actions.
highlights: Local model downloads in Settings – Local Providers now list recommended Ollama and LM Studio models with hardware guidance and quick download or copy actions.
More reliable local model output – Local prompts now enforce template rules so assembled filenames match transformed fields, and JSON/fenced responses are cleaned automatically.
Cleaner freeform naming on local providers – Ollama and LM Studio freeform authoring now uses chat endpoints for more consistent responses.
Suffixes now stick with dot separators. Smart Rename no longer drops suffix text when you use . as the separator.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.01
releases.excerpt: Instant Search – Press ⌘F to search across all settings. Select a result to jump directly to that setting, highlighted and ready to adjust.
highlights: Instant Search – Press ⌘F to search across all settings. Select a result to jump directly to that setting, highlighted and ready to adjust.
Keyboard Navigation – Use ⌘1 through ⌘6 to jump to any category instantly.
Refreshed AI Provider Cards – Each provider displays its official logo with clear connection status indicators.
More List Columns – Six new column options: Last Viewed, Created Date, Modified Date, File Size, Tags, and Comments. Choose up to 3 to display.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.00
releases.excerpt: Try NameQuick Free with Your Own API Key – New users can now try NameQuick free for 14 days using their own OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or Ollama API key. The trial includes a set number of AI-powered renames to help you evaluate the app before committing.
highlights: Try NameQuick Free with Your Own API Key – New users can now try NameQuick free for 14 days using their own OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or Ollama API key. The trial includes a set number of AI-powered renames to help you evaluate the app before committing.

Subscription Trials for New Customers – If you prefer not to use your own API key, you can now start a free trial of our managed AI service. Existing customers returning after a break will see a welcome-back flow that recognizes their account.

Faster, More Memory-Efficient Image Processing – Renamed files containing images now process more efficiently with lower memory usage. Large batches of screenshots and photos should no longer cause slowdowns or high memory consumption.

More Reliable Gemini Integration – Files that Gemini cannot process (due to content restrictions) now display a clear error message instead of retrying silently. Credits are only charged when a rename actually succeeds.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.09
releases.excerpt: - Fixed an issue where template-based renaming could produce generic filenames like _.pdf instead of extracting meaningful names from your documents. Templates now reliably capture the fields you've defined.
highlights: - Fixed an issue where template-based renaming could produce generic filenames like _.pdf instead of extracting meaningful names from your documents. Templates now reliably capture the fields you've defined.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.08
releases.excerpt: - Fixed a rare issue where subscription credits could stay “stuck” on a device after your billing period renewed or your plan changed.
highlights: - Fixed a rare issue where subscription credits could stay “stuck” on a device after your billing period renewed or your plan changed.

- Fixed an issue where custom prompts that analyze screenshots or images would sometimes ignore the visual content and only consider text. These prompts now correctly take the image itself into account when generating results.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.07
releases.excerpt: Added sorting options to the toolbar. You can now sort files by name, date, size, or type in ascending or descending order.
highlights: Added sorting options to the toolbar. You can now sort files by name, date, size, or type in ascending or descending order.

Fixed an issue where custom separators in naming patterns (like dashes, underscores, or spaces) could be lost when reopening or duplicating a pattern. Your patterns now preserve exactly the separators you chose.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.06
releases.excerpt: Fixed custom separators with spaces (like " - ") being stripped down to just the character (e.g., "-"). Filenames now correctly preserve space-surrounded separators for formats like "2024-01-15 - Invoice.pdf".
highlights: Fixed custom separators with spaces (like " - ") being stripped down to just the character (e.g., "-"). Filenames now correctly preserve space-surrounded separators for formats like "2024-01-15 - Invoice.pdf".
Fixed filename patterns dropping custom literal text. Any static words you add (e.g., "Test") now stay in the assembled filename alongside your extracted fields.
Fixed template testing failures caused by overly strict validation of AI response audit logs. Processing now correctly succeeds when all required fields are extracted.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.05
releases.excerpt: Fixed issue where duplicate files could incorrectly receive the same AI-generated name as a previously processed file.
highlights: Fixed issue where duplicate files could incorrectly receive the same AI-generated name as a previously processed file.
Fixed template preview incorrectly showing "Could not extract" errors when fields were actually extracted successfully.
Fixed sidebar disappearing after closing Settings.
Added tooltips to sidebar tabs explaining their purpose (All Files, To Process, Processed).

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.04
releases.excerpt: Improved reliability for managed AI processing.
highlights: Improved reliability for managed AI processing.
Added recovery handling for corrupted template files with a clear warning when detected.
Fixed issue where file renames could appear successful without actually renaming the file.
Fixed processing delay setting not saving between sessions.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.03
releases.excerpt: Fixed an issue where auto-processing with Custom Prompts would ignore document content, resulting in poor rename quality.
highlights: Fixed an issue where auto-processing with Custom Prompts would ignore document content, resulting in poor rename quality.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.02
releases.excerpt: Fixed a bug where Custom Prompts created in earlier versions weren't visible after updating to v2.10.00 or v2.10.01. Your prompts are automatically restored on launch.
highlights: Fixed a bug where Custom Prompts created in earlier versions weren't visible after updating to v2.10.00 or v2.10.01. Your prompts are automatically restored on launch.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.01
releases.excerpt: Fixed an issue where some users were shown the onboarding flow again after restarting the app, even though they had already completed it.
highlights: Fixed an issue where some users were shown the onboarding flow again after restarting the app, even though they had already completed it.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.00
releases.excerpt: Visual pattern builder with drag-and-drop chips that show exactly how your filename will look
highlights: Visual pattern builder with drag-and-drop chips that show exactly how your filename will look
Per-field separators so you can mix dashes, underscores, and spaces however you need
Live preview updates as you build, so you always know what you're getting
Test your templates while building and iterate until they're perfect

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.47
releases.excerpt: Another small bug fix for subscription users.
highlights: Another small bug fix for subscription users.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.46
releases.excerpt: Small bug fix for subscription users.
highlights: Small bug fix for subscription users.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.45
releases.excerpt: Fixed a license logging crash when disk space is low by serializing writes on an actor and gating the HUD to foreground state.
highlights: Fixed a license logging crash when disk space is low by serializing writes on an actor and gating the HUD to foreground state.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.44
releases.excerpt: Credits now properly refresh when your subscription billing cycle renews. Previously, some users saw their old credit balance until they logged out and back in.
highlights: Credits now properly refresh when your subscription billing cycle renews. Previously, some users saw their old credit balance until they logged out and back in.
Cached credit data is now automatically invalidated when a new billing period begins, ensuring you always see your current balance.
Large PDFs now process more reliably. Increased internal compression target from 15 MB to 45 MB, allowing high-resolution scanned documents to succeed where they previously failed.
Oversized PDFs now show a clear error message instead of failing silently. If a PDF exceeds the 50 MB processing limit, you'll see exactly what the problem is and how to fix it.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.43
releases.excerpt: NameQuick 2.0.43 —
highlights: NameQuick 2.0.43 —
Smoother BYOK licensing
What’s fixed - Removes the “license validation” warning HUD that
could briefly appear on launch for customers using BYOK/Local license

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.42
releases.excerpt: NameQuick
highlights: NameQuick
2.0.42 — Reliable renames on macOS 26.1
What’s fixed - Fixed a rare issue where some files couldn’t be opened
or renamed after using NameQuick on macOS 26.1. - Files renamed with

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.41
releases.excerpt: Small fixes and improvements
highlights: Small fixes and improvements

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.40
releases.excerpt: Fix: resolves a rare freeze on macOS Sonoma where the sidebar repeatedly hid and showed, spiking CPU and stalling the app. NameQuick now opens and stays responsive.
highlights: Fix: resolves a rare freeze on macOS Sonoma where the sidebar repeatedly hid and showed, spiking CPU and stalling the app. NameQuick now opens and stays responsive.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.39
releases.excerpt: Smarter duplicate-name handling: when a target filename already exists, NameQuick now automatically appends a counter (e.g., ” (2)“) and completes the rename, instead of showing a”file already exists” error. This keeps your flow uninterrupted and preserves the original extension.
highlights: Smarter duplicate-name handling: when a target filename already exists, NameQuick now automatically appends a counter (e.g., ” (2)“) and completes the rename, instead of showing a”file already exists” error. This keeps your flow uninterrupted and preserves the original extension.

Gemini model updates (current + compatible): the selector now shows the latest 2.5 and 2.0 language models that work with NameQuick’s generateContent flow, and removes legacy/unsupported entries. Added: Gemini 2.5 Pro, 2.5 Flash, 2.5 Flash‑Lite, 2.5 Flash‑Lite (Preview 09‑2025); Gemini 2.0 Flash, 2.0 Flash‑Lite, 2.0 Flash (Experimental).
Removed: Gemini 1.5/1.0 families, Live‑only, and TTS models (not compatible with this flow).
Automatic migration: if you previously selected a removed model (e.g., 1.5/1.0, Live preview, old Flash‑Lite preview ID), you’ll switch to the closest 2.5 equivalent on launch and in the selector.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.38
releases.excerpt: Implemented a bug fix focusing on size limitations experienced by a user
highlights: Implemented a bug fix focusing on size limitations experienced by a user
Some crash fixes

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.37
releases.excerpt: Large PDF uploads are now processed reliably without memory spikes. We preprocess PDFs before reading them, keeping memory use low and preventing crashes on very large files.
highlights: Large PDF uploads are now processed reliably without memory spikes. We preprocess PDFs before reading them, keeping memory use low and preventing crashes on very large files.
Fixed vertical overflow issues in the Settings view for improved layout and accessibility
General stability improvements and crash fixes

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.36
releases.excerpt: Drag to reorder folders in the sidebar
highlights: Drag to reorder folders in the sidebar
Exclude Subfolders now sticks reliably
Re-adding removed folders works immediately (no extra permission steps)
Better filename fidelity and control Parentheses and square brackets are now preserved in names (e.g., “(Company)”, “[25 AUG ’25]”).

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.35
releases.excerpt: Per‑folder subfolder control: include or exclude subfolders for each watch folder from the sidebar context menu.
highlights: Per‑folder subfolder control: include or exclude subfolders for each watch folder from the sidebar context menu.
Right‑click a watch folder in the sidebar → toggle “Include Subfolders”.
Fixed an issue where custom prompts did not run when dragging and dropping a file into the app in subscription mode.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.34
releases.excerpt: Added a global Clean filenames toggle under Preferences → General & Permissions → Defaults. When enabled, NameQuick transliterates German umlauts (ä→ae, ö→oe, ü→ue, Ä→Ae, Ö→Oe, Ü→Ue, ß→ss) and keeps only letters, numbers, single spaces, underscores, and dashes across Smart Rename, Templates, and Custom Prompt flows.
highlights: Added a global Clean filenames toggle under Preferences → General & Permissions → Defaults. When enabled, NameQuick transliterates German umlauts (ä→ae, ö→oe, ü→ue, Ä→Ae, Ö→Oe, Ü→Ue, ß→ss) and keeps only letters, numbers, single spaces, underscores, and dashes across Smart Rename, Templates, and Custom Prompt flows.
Updated the rename preview and documentation to call out the exact cleanup rules so teams know what stays and what goes before uploading files.

Replaced the credit usage queue with a GRDB-backed worker that keeps retries off the main thread, prevents runaway JSON re-encoding, and eliminates the high-CPU watchdog terminations some users saw when Polar was slow.
Removed the deprecated Smart Rename and Basic settings tabs now that the Craft-style sidebar covers those options.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.32
releases.excerpt: Drag & Drop and Global Shortcut : Both operations now consistently apply prefixes and suffixes. ### UI Layout Improvements
highlights: Drag & Drop and Global Shortcut : Both operations now consistently apply prefixes and suffixes. ### UI Layout Improvements
Settings Window Responsiveness : Fixed layout overflow issues at compact window widths.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.31
releases.excerpt: Claude BYOK pacing – Batch rename jobs automatically throttle requests so you can push more files without tripping Anthropic’s burst limits or seeing 429 errors.
highlights: Claude BYOK pacing – Batch rename jobs automatically throttle requests so you can push more files without tripping Anthropic’s burst limits or seeing 429 errors.

Managed prompt creation – Fixed a crash that occurred when Managed (Plug & Play) subscribers pressed “New Prompt.” The add/edit form now opens inline instead of stacking a second sheet, so the NSWindowAlreadyHasSheetException path is gone.

Watch folder stability – Eliminated duplicate watch-folder entries that caused _MergeError crashes at launch, especially on macOS Sequoia. Saved profiles are cleaned automatically and add/remove now handle path variants safely.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.30
releases.excerpt: Fixed false routing in subscription mode
highlights: Fixed false routing in subscription mode

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.29
releases.excerpt: License activation no longer crashes. If something goes wrong while activating, you’ll just see the warning message.
highlights: License activation no longer crashes. If something goes wrong while activating, you’ll just see the warning message.
Connectivity validation keeps running. Skipping a recent success no longer refreshes the cache, so periodic checks still execute and catch environment/token issues.
Credit balance sync now surfaces instantly on launch. Cached snapshots load immediately while Polar refresh runs in the background, eliminating the multi-second stall when reopening the app.
Reduced Polar auth chatter. Successful connectivity checks are cached for 10 minutes so relaunches skip the redundant HEAD request that previously blocked credit syncing.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.28
releases.excerpt: Custom prompts moved to Processing settings. Find “Manage Custom Prompts…” inside Settings → Processing ### Bug Fixes
highlights: Custom prompts moved to Processing settings. Find “Manage Custom Prompts…” inside Settings → Processing ### Bug Fixes
Crash on launch eliminated. The Accessibility relaunch prompt now always runs on the main actor, so fresh installs and permission changes no longer trip a Swift precondition and terminate the app.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.27
releases.excerpt: Quick actions stay visible even before you select files. The floating process bar no longer disappears when you load a batch, making it obvious how to kick off renames and when actions are disabled versus ready to run.
highlights: Quick actions stay visible even before you select files. The floating process bar no longer disappears when you load a batch, making it obvious how to kick off renames and when actions are disabled versus ready to run.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.26
releases.excerpt: Never fall back to BYOK for managed accounts. If we’re momentarily offline from Polar, NameQuick now keeps your Managed AI license active, works from cached access, and automatically returns to the managed Gemini service once reconnected.
highlights: Never fall back to BYOK for managed accounts. If we’re momentarily offline from Polar, NameQuick now keeps your Managed AI license active, works from cached access, and automatically returns to the managed Gemini service once reconnected.

Clear warning and logging when service is offline. You’ll see a brief “Managed AI is temporarily offline…” banner if we’re working from cached access, and the diagnostics log captures the fallback event so support can diagnose it quickly.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.25
releases.excerpt: Adaptive menu bar icon – The menu bar symbol now respects light and dark appearances, keeping contrast consistent even when the system theme switches mid-session.
highlights: Adaptive menu bar icon – The menu bar symbol now respects light and dark appearances, keeping contrast consistent even when the system theme switches mid-session.
Quickly hide the menu bar icon – Added a Show Menu Bar Icon toggle in Appearance and General settings so you can run NameQuick without the status-item when you prefer a cleaner menu bar.
Correct vision model command – Updated the “Add Vision Model” action in Settings so it now copies ollama run qwen2.5vl:32b , ensuring the suggested Ollama command works without manual correction.
No other changes in this build; this release focuses on menu bar polish and one Ollama setup fix.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.24
releases.excerpt: ## Features
highlights: ## Features
## Fixes

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.23
releases.excerpt: New queue management popover – Introducing a dedicated queue popover where you can view all your queued rename files. Remove individual items or clear the entire queue with a single click—everything updates instantly with live badge counts.
highlights: New queue management popover – Introducing a dedicated queue popover where you can view all your queued rename files. Remove individual items or clear the entire queue with a single click—everything updates instantly with live badge counts.
Editing made stable – Fixed an issue where the app would crash when trying to edit your default naming template. You can now safely customize all your templates without any hiccups.

License management simplified – Resolved a quirk where the app would sometimes re-activate itself after you’d reset your license. Once you unlink your license, it stays unlinked until you choose to activate it again.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.22
releases.excerpt: Redesigned for macOS Tahoe – Beautiful new user interface optimized for Apple’s latest macOS, with modern visual refinements throughout the app.
highlights: Redesigned for macOS Tahoe – Beautiful new user interface optimized for Apple’s latest macOS, with modern visual refinements throughout the app.
Vector artists rejoice – Drop in AI, SVG, or EPS files and NameQuick will import, preview, and rename them just like your photos.
Templates that feel native – The Templates screen now looks and behaves like Finder. Quick actions sit right where you expect them, and the pencil button jumps straight into editing.
Memory leak fixed – Eliminated a critical memory leak that could cause crashes during extended use—the app now runs reliably for hours without issues.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.21
releases.excerpt: Before Rename : Rules evaluate files as they enter watch folders, perfect for initial routing and organization
highlights: Before Rename : Rules evaluate files as they enter watch folders, perfect for initial routing and organization
After Rename : Rules can run again after AI completes renaming, allowing you to organize files based on their final names
{original} - Original filename without extension
{date_taken} - EXIF date taken or creation date

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.20
releases.excerpt: ## Features
highlights: ## Features
## Fixes

highlights: v2.0.19 — Create Your Own
Your naming rules, automated.
You can now write custom prompts for how you want files renamed. Pick
one from the toolbar, and NameQuick will use it for every file until you

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.18
releases.excerpt: All‑new onboarding flow with just‑in‑time prompts and a faster path to your first rename.
highlights: All‑new onboarding flow with just‑in‑time prompts and a faster path to your first rename.
Undo renames from grid view, list view, and the Mac menu bar.
Cleaner Mac menu bar UI.
The processing spinner is now in the Mac menu bar.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.17
releases.excerpt: Email login: Addresses like name+tag@domain.com now work as expected.
highlights: Email login: Addresses like name+tag@domain.com now work as expected.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.16
releases.excerpt: No more login Keychain prompts when viewing AI settings.
highlights: No more login Keychain prompts when viewing AI settings.
Smoother settings experience with non‑interactive Keychain reads.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.15
releases.excerpt: Templates (Claude): Reliable structured output with consistent results.
highlights: Templates (Claude): Reliable structured output with consistent results.
OpenAI schema: Fixes rare “type: None” error in structured output.
Spreadsheets: Stable memory + faster previews for large XLSX files.
Error messages: Clearer, provider‑specific guidance.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.14
releases.excerpt: New: You can now rename Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (DOCX, PPTX, XLSX) .
highlights: New: You can now rename Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (DOCX, PPTX, XLSX) .
BYOK: Claude (Anthropic) joins the lineup with native PDF + image analysis and explicit model selection.
Word (.docx): Smart, clean names for your documents — fast previews included.
PowerPoint (.pptx): Uses slide text to suggest clear context-aware names.
